title: Grant - Canada.ca
content:
## Granting citizenship
This section contains policy, procedures and guidance used by IRCC staff. It is posted on the department’s website as a courtesy to stakeholders.
This section describes the requirements and the process of applying for different types of grants of Canadian citizenship.
- Adults who are 18 to 54 years of age
- Adults who are 55 years of age or older
- Minors applying under subsection 5(2)
- Timing of applications for minors
- Minor applying under subsection 5(1)
- Citizenship knowledge and language assessment: Tests and hearings
- Conducting interviews and hearings with minors
- Stateless individuals
- Canadian Armed Forces
- Residence/Physical Presence
- CP 14: Adoptions ( PDF , 1.23 MB )
- Travel documents for a grant of citizenship for an adopted person
- Waivers on compassionate grounds
- Automatic acquisition of citizenship
- Resumption of citizenship
- Resumption of citizenship: Canadian Armed Forces
